content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "ui-design-system",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 62,
    "domain_depth": 45,
    "data_richness": 40,
    "interaction_quality": 55,
    "ai_integration": 10,
    "mobile_first": 48,
    "performance": 58,
    "typescript_quality": 52,
    "error_handling": 55,
    "realtime": 30,
    "accessibility": 42,
    "security": 50,
    "differentiation": 40,
    "enterprise_readiness": 45
  },
  "overall_score": 45,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Toast notifications missing ARIA live region container and keyboard dismiss",
      "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx",
      "description": "ToastProvider renders toasts but the container wrapping all toasts lacks role='region' and aria-label. Individual toasts have aria-live but the parent container is a bare div. Screen readers may not announce toasts reliably. The dismiss button also lacks visible focus ring styling.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastProvider component (or wherever the toast container div wraps the list of ToastEntry components): BEFORE: The container div that maps over toasts has no ARIA attributes and the dismiss button has no focus-visible outline. AFTER: Add role='region' and aria-label='Notifications' to the toast container div. On the dismiss button inside ToastEntry, add a style for outline: `outline: 'none'` normally and use onFocus/onBlur state or a CSS class to show `outline: '2px solid ' + colors.primary` and `outlineOffset: '2px'` on focus-visible. Also ensure the toast container has `position: 'fixed', bottom: spacing['4'], right: spacing['4'], zIndex: zIndex.toast || 9999, display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', gap: spacing['2']` if not already present. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Breadcrumbs overflow on mobile with no truncation fallback",
      "file": "src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx",
      "description": "FolderBreadcrumbs truncation logic exists but the route-based breadcrumb (used on main pages) has no mobile handling at all. On screens under 768px with deep navigation, breadcrumbs overflow the viewport horizontally. No overflow-x scroll or ellipsis collapse is applied to the main route breadcrumb nav.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the main route-based breadcrumb component (not FolderBreadcrumbs, but whatever renders the top-level page breadcrumbs using routeLabels): BEFORE: The nav element for route breadcrumbs has no overflow handling, causing horizontal overflow on mobile. AFTER: Add `overflowX: 'auto'` and `maxWidth: '100%'` to the nav container. Add `flexShrink: 0` to each breadcrumb item. Add `WebkitOverflowScrolling: 'touch'` for iOS momentum scrolling. Also add a CSS scrollbar-hide style by setting `scrollbarWidth: 'none'` and `msOverflowStyle: 'none'` on the nav. If the component doesn't exist yet as a separate export, add the overflow styles to FolderBreadcrumbs' nav element as well by ensuring `overflowX: 'auto'` and `flexWrap: 'nowrap'` are set. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "No global error boundary wrapping the app shell",
      "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx",
      "description": "The UI design system provides Toast and ContextMenu but there is no ErrorBoundary component exported from the design system module. If a child component throws during render, the entire app crashes to a white screen instead of showing a recovery UI with 'Something went wrong' and a retry button.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, at the bottom of the file (or create a new section after the ToastProvider export): Add a class-based ErrorBoundary component. BEFORE: No ErrorBoundary exists in the design system. AFTER: Export a React class component called ErrorBoundary with state `{ hasError: boolean; error: Error | null }`. Implement static getDerivedStateFromError to set hasError true. In render, if hasError, return a centered div with: a warning icon (use a simple SVG or unicode ⚠️), heading 'Something went wrong' (styled with typography.fontSize.lg, colors.textPrimary, fontWeight 600), the error message in colors.textTertiary at fontSize.sm, and a button styled with the primary button pattern (background colors.primary, color white, borderRadius.md, padding spacing[2] spacing[4], cursor pointer, border none) that calls `this.setState({ hasError: false, error: null })` with text 'Try Again'. The wrapper div should have `display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'center', minHeight: '400px', gap: spacing[4], padding: spacing[6], fontFamily: typography.fontFamily`. If no error, render this.props.children. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "visual_polish",
      "title": "Theme tokens not enforced — hardcoded color values in component styles",
      "file": "src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx",
      "description": "The Breadcrumbs component uses theme tokens from the import but several inline style patterns across the codebase may use raw hex values instead of theme tokens. In FolderBreadcrumbs, the hover color logic uses string interpolation that could drift from the theme. The design standards mandate no hardcoded colors. Need to audit and ensure all color references use the colors object.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component and any hover/active state handlers: BEFORE: Some color values may be string literals or computed values not referencing the theme colors object (e.g., hover backgrounds computed as rgba strings or any raw hex like '#F47820'). AFTER: Ensure every color reference uses the imported `colors` object. Replace any hardcoded hover background with `colors.surfaceHover` or `colors.bgTertiary`. Replace any hardcoded text color with `colors.textPrimary`, `colors.textSecondary`, or `colors.textTertiary`. Replace any hardcoded active/focus color with `colors.primary`. For the chevron separator icon color, use `colors.textTertiary`. For the home icon, use `colors.textSecondary`. Verify no raw hex strings remain in the file. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "ContextMenu has no keyboard navigation support",
      "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx",
      "description": "The ContextMenu component likely renders a dropdown menu on right-click or button click, but there is no onKeyDown handler for arrow key navigation between menu items, Enter/Space to select, or Escape to close. This fails the design standards requirement for keyboard shortcuts and the accessibility global standard for keyboard navigability.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ContextMenu component (the right-click or dropdown menu component): BEFORE: Menu items are only clickable with mouse. No keyboard event handlers exist for navigating between items or closing the menu. AFTER: Add a `onKeyDown` handler to the menu container div. Track a `focusedIndex` state (number, default -1). On ArrowDown, increment focusedIndex (wrap to 0 at end). On ArrowUp, decrement (wrap to last item). On Enter or Space, trigger the onClick of the focused item. On Escape, close the menu. Apply `role='menu'` to the container and `role='menuitem'` plus `tabIndex={focusedIndex === index ? 0 : -1}` to each item. Use a useEffect to focus the element at focusedIndex when it changes (via ref array). Set `aria-activedescendant` on the menu container to the id of the focused item. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's UI uses a mature design system with consistent tokens, ARIA compliance, and responsive layouts. Fieldwire excels at mobile-first touch interactions. SiteSync's design system has theme tokens defined but enforcement is inconsistent. The Toast system exists but lacks full accessibility. No ErrorBoundary means a single component crash kills the entire app, which is unacceptable for field use. Keyboard navigation gaps put SiteSync behind both Procore and Linear in interaction quality.",
  "summary": "The UI Design System module has foundational elements in place — theme tokens, a Toast notification system, Breadcrumbs, and ContextMenu — but enforcement and completeness are lacking. The most critical gaps are: no ErrorBoundary (any render error crashes the app to white screen), incomplete ARIA attributes on the Toast system (screen readers may miss notifications), no keyboard navigation on ContextMenu, mobile overflow issues on Breadcrumbs, and potential hardcoded colors bypassing theme tokens. These five issues directly violate the Global Acceptance Standards for accessibility, error states, mobile responsiveness, and design consistency. Fixing them requires minimal code changes but delivers outsized impact on reliability and usability. The module scores 45 overall because while the architecture is sound, the execution gaps mean the design system cannot yet serve as the reliable foundation that every other module depends on."
}
```
